title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Prompt Defense
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a safe tool-use decision protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.
Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Use a changed quiz case for prompt tools actions that tests this competency: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

stimulus.title: Tool Failure Recovery failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

options.text: Accept the Tool Failure Recovery result because visible a safe tool-use decision protocol output is the highest authority. This does not resolve diagnose the hidden failure.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Tool Failure Recovery without its stated preconditions, omit the a safe tool-use decision protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Tool Failure Recovery for a safe tool-use decision protocol.
Run the Tool Failure Recovery boundary case for a safe tool-use decision protocol, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Tool Failure Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed quiz case for prompt tools actions that tests this competency: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.. steps.why: Retrieving Tool Failure Recovery through the concrete a safe tool-use decision protocol case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.
